Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 13:02 ET
Healthcare options explode on M&A speculation while tech divergence deepens pre-CPI
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Healthcare names dominate unusual call flow led by TERN's 270% surge and managed care plays, suggesting sector M&A activity accelerating.
  • 2. The P/C split widens to extreme levels — index puts at 1.82 while single-names show call preference at 0.98, classic dispersion setup into CPI.
  • 3. Negative $8.2B SPY gamma amplifies any CPI-driven moves with tech showing dangerous vol/flow mismatches.
NARRATIVE — negative gamma amplifies CPI risk

Healthcare options are pricing in accelerated M&A activity, with TERN leading unusual call flow at +270% volume and managed care names UNH (+93%) and HUM (+58%) following suit. The concentration in healthcare calls suggests sector-specific catalysts beyond broader market positioning, likely driven by regulatory clarity expectations and valuation compression creating acquisition opportunities. The P/C divergence has reached extreme levels that signal sophisticated hedging ahead of Wednesday's CPI print — index puts heavy at 1.82 while single-name calls dominate at 0.98. This classic dispersion structure reflects institutional preference to hedge broad market risk while maintaining single-name upside exposure. With SPY's negative $8.2B gamma environment, any CPI surprise will be amplified through dealer hedging flows. Tech presents the most dangerous cross-currents into the data release, with six names showing vol high/skew flat divergences including AAPL and MSFT. Despite Millennium's aggressive Q4 adds in MSFT (+135% to $2.49B) and AMZN's 398% reversal, options flow shows rich vol/call heavy mismatches in TMO and INTU that could unwind violently on any growth concerns from the inflation data.

Layer 1: Sentiment -6Layer 2: GEX -$8.2BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.82/0.98
